title: Jerry Donathan is the Pro-Abortion Candidate in SD7
author: Sam Riley
orientation: An abolitionist critique of Oklahoma SD7 Democratic candidate Jerry Donathan, arguing that his rape, incest, health, and life-of-the-mother exceptions make him a pro-abortion candidate functionally indistinguishable from Roe v. Wade. The piece urges voters to reject Donathan and support Republican Warren Hamilton instead.
audiences: Oklahoma SD7 voters weighing the Hamilton vs. Donathan race
Pro-life advocates unfamiliar with the legal mechanics of Roe and Doe v. Bolton
Pastors and church leaders advising congregants on voting

questions.question: Why does Free the States classify Jerry Donathan as a pro-abortion candidate rather than a moderate?
questions.audience: Oklahoma SD7 voters weighing the Hamilton vs. Donathan race
questions.answer: Because Donathan publicly endorses rape, incest, health, and life-of-the-mother exceptions, which under the legal framework of Roe and Doe v. Bolton functionally permit all abortions. Sam Riley argues this position is identical to that of prominent national Democrats and amounts to defending the pro-abortion status quo, regardless of how Donathan frames it.
questions.quote: Jerry Donathan thinks he can trick the voters of Senate District 7 into thinking he’s some sort of pro-life with exceptions candidate who deserves the support of people who care about vulnerable preborn children, but his position is no different than Diane Feinstein, Alexandra Ocasio Cortez, or Joe Biden – namely that *Roe* (ie. legal murder) is the law and we must allow the poisoning, dismemberment, and decapitation or preborn humans in our state.

questions.question: What specific exceptions did Donathan say he supports in his campaign video?
questions.audience: Oklahoma SD7 voters weighing the Hamilton vs. Donathan race
questions.answer: Donathan named four exceptions: rape, incest, the health of the woman, and the life of the woman. The article emphasizes that he treated 'health' and 'life' as separate categories, which the author considers legally and morally significant.
questions.quote: Donathan said, “I feel that in the cases of rape, incest, *health*, and *life* of the woman should be considered as exceptions.”

questions.question: Why does the article insist that 'life of the mother' is not a legitimate medical justification for abortion?
questions.answer: Riley contends that medical professionals—pointing to the Dublin Declaration—have rebutted the notion that intentionally killing a preborn child is ever required to save a mother's life. He argues alternative treatments consistent with the Hippocratic oath always exist.
questions.quote: There is absolutely no warrant to the claim that intentionally murdering a baby is a necessary treatment for saving women’s lives, even when doctors prescribe it. There are always other treatments that are in line with the Hippocratic oath.

questions.question: How does Doe v. Bolton's definition of 'health' function as a loophole legalizing abortion on demand?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocates unfamiliar with the legal mechanics of Roe and Doe v. Bolton
questions.answer: The Supreme Court defined 'health' so expansively—covering physical, emotional, psychological, familial, and age-related factors—that virtually any pregnancy qualifies. Riley argues this is how abortion was effectively legalized in all 50 states, since pregnancy hormones alone create emotional effects that satisfy the standard.
questions.quote: In the accompanying case of [Doe v. Bolton](https://caselaw.findlaw.com/us-supreme-court/410/179.html), which was decided the same day as Roe, the court defined health of the mother as, “all factors – physical, emotional, psychological, familial, and the woman’s age-relevant to the wellbeing of the patient. All these factors may relate to health.” In case you can’t see the sinister nature of this definition, what this means is that if a woman is emotionally affected by a pregnancy, which all women are because of the associated hormones, she can get an abortion.

questions.question: On what moral grounds does the article reject the rape and incest exception?
questions.answer: Riley invokes the principle that children should not be punished for their fathers' crimes, calling it unjust to impose 'capital punishment' on a preborn child because of how he was conceived. The exception alone, he argues, is sufficient reason to reject Donathan.
questions.quote: If it was only for his position that you can stab a baby to death because his father is a rapist it would be more than enough reason to never vote for him. A child should never be given a capital punishment for the sins of his father.

questions.question: What does the article frame as the actual choice facing SD7 voters?
questions.audience: Pastors and church leaders advising congregants on voting
questions.answer: Riley presents a binary: voting Donathan perpetuates legalized 'child sacrifice' in Oklahoma, while voting Warren Hamilton supports a 'principled conservative and anti-abortion candidate' who would work to override the Supreme Court and end abortion in the state.
questions.quote: The choice is clear: If you want child sacrifice to remain legal in Oklahoma, vote for Jerry Donathan. If you want equal justice and protection to be upheld for all image bearers of God in Oklahoma, vote for Warren Hamilton, a principled conservative and anti-abortion candidate who will work to check the abuses perpetrated by the supreme court and bring an end to the slaughter of preborn children in the state of Oklahoma.

questions.question: How does the article characterize the rhetorical strategy Donathan used to win pro-life votes?
questions.answer: Riley accuses Donathan of using vague language to appear moderate—claiming opposition to abortion while politically supporting it—in order to peel off voters who oppose abortion but don't understand how Roe was constructed.
questions.quote: In an attempt to appear moderate on abortion and entice voters who are against it, he stated in vague language that he was essentially pro-abortion politically while trying to maintain that he was against it.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists for Abolishing Abortion Endorses Warren Hamilton for SD7
related.reason: Direct endorsement of Donathan's opponent in the same Senate District 7 race.

related.title: Free Will Baptist Abolitionist Leaders and Pastors Endorse Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Additional endorsements bolstering the article's case for Hamilton over Donathan.

related.title: Warren Hamilton Defeats Larry Boggs, Margie Alfonso Defeats Clay Iiams
related.reason: Reports Hamilton's primary win that set up the general election contest with Donathan.

related.title: Warren Hamilton Wins!
related.reason: Reports the actual general election outcome of the Hamilton vs. Donathan race covered here.

related.title: Disgraceful! Pro-Life Establishment Lies About Abolitionist Candidate Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Companion piece defending Hamilton against attacks during the same campaign cycle.

related.title: Here's What We're Asking Oklahoma Candidates
related.reason: Frames the abolitionist litmus test that Donathan fails and Hamilton passes.

related.title: No Exceptions
related.reason: Provides the broader theological and ethical case against the rape, incest, health, and life-of-the-mother exceptions Donathan endorses.
